title: tests: run conformance test suite in Travis
body:
Follow up to #1003

Signed-off-by: Lokesh Mandvekar <lsm5@fedoraproject.org>

@TomSweeneyRedHat @nalind @rhatdan @mrunalp PTAL.
This currently takes < 15 mins on my fork.

comments.body: no idea whatsup, travis simply refuses to start on this.
Travis seems busted for buildah. Also happening on https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/1031
but it's working for my fork o_O

so Travis finally ran and it's failing on conformance tests. I could disable the whole `run stuff in Fedora container` until we have tests fixed. Or would you prefer we run through all the tests that pass and disable the ones that fail for now?

On Thu, Sep 20, 2018 at 10:09:09AM -0700, Tom Sweeney wrote:
> TomSweeneyRedHat commented on this pull request.
> 
> 
> 
> > -echo "buildah bud dockerfile..."
> -./buildah --registries-conf tests/registries.conf bud --file hack/Dockerfile -t buildah-test-image .
> -
> -echo "buildah tag buildah-test-image..."
> -./buildah tag buildah-test-image localhost:5000/buildah-test-image
> -
> -echo "buildah push buildah-test-image..."
> -./buildah push --cert-dir /home/travis/auth --tls-verify=false --authfile /root/.docker/config.json buildah-test-image localhost:5000/buildah-test-image
> -
> -echo "docker pull buildah-test-image..."
> -docker pull localhost:5000/buildah-test-image
> -
> -echo "Running container-diff..."
> -container-diff diff --type=rpm daemon://localhost:5000/buildah-test-image daemon://docker-test-image
> +echo "Running conformance tests..."
> +GOPATH=$(pwd) go test -v ./tests/conformance
> 
> Are the removed *test-image tests taken care of elsewhere?

So, I was under the impression that tests/conformance handled all those cases
already, that's why the deletion of these lines. Was that a wrong assumption?


-- 
Lokesh
IRC: lsm5
GPG: 0xC7C3A0DD
https://keybase.io/lsm5

@lsm5 answering your question, I'm not 100% sure they're handled by the conformance tests.  Given that and the conformance test is likely to be turned off to start, I'd rather keep them unless you think it's unwise or completely duplicitous.   I'm fine with a TODO comment in there to remove them after the conformance tests go up fully if you think we're covered by that.

@lsm5 I'm fine with that once the conformance tests are running properly.  Short term I'd like to leave them as is.  Noting the conformance tests are probably fine, the code needs some tweaking that's in progress.
Let's resume this PR once tests are fixed in that case. Thanks @TomSweeneyRedHat
@lsm5 I am fine with this, but @TomSweeneyRedHat has one last question that needs answering before we merge.
@TomSweeneyRedHat so the latest travis run does run the test but doesn't block on it. Let me know if there are any other unresolved issues.
@TomSweeneyRedHat also, `hack/Dockerfile` and related runs have been restored.
@TomSweeneyRedHat Merge this if you are happy with it.
LGTM @lsm5, thanks for getting this in and TY @ypu for putting together the conformance tests!
Homu does not seem to be running.  Merging manually.
